title: sambucus williamsii subsp. coreana
intro: Sambucus williamsii subsp. coreana, also known as the Korean elder or Korean red elder, is a plant species within the elderberry genus Sambucus. It is native to Korea and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. The plant's berries, flowers, and leaves have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to their rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins. Sambucus williamsii subsp. coreana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a natural remedy for colds, flu, and other viral infections.
